moduleDescription:
An implementation of a deque (double-ended queue). The underlying implementation uses a seq.
Note: None of the procs that get an individual value from the deque should be used on an empty deque.

If compiled with the boundChecks option, those procs will raise an IndexDefect on such access. This should not be relied upon, as -d:danger or --checks:off will disable those checks and then the procs may return garbage or crash the program.

As such, a check to see if the deque is empty is needed before any access, unless your program logic guarantees it indirectly.

See also

  • lists module for singly and doubly linked lists and rings

entries.name: Deque
entries.code: Deque[T] = object
entries.description:
A double-ended queue backed with a ringed seq buffer.

To initialize an empty deque, use the initDeque proc.

entries.name: defaultInitialSize
entries.code: defaultInitialSize = 4

entries.name: initDeque
entries.code: proc initDeque[T](initialSize: int = defaultInitialSize): Deque[T]
entries.description:
Creates a new empty deque.

Optionally, the initial capacity can be reserved via initialSize as a performance optimization (default: defaultInitialSize). The length of a newly created deque will still be 0.

See also:

  • toDeque proc

entries.name: len
entries.code: func len[T](deq: Deque[T]): int {.inline.}
entries.description: Returns the number of elements of deq.

entries.code: proc `[]`[T](deq: Deque[T]; i: Natural): lent T {.inline.}
entries.description: Accesses the i-th element of deq.

entries.code: proc `[]`[T](deq: var Deque[T]; i: Natural): var T {.inline.}
entries.description: Accesses the i-th element of deq and returns a mutable reference to it.

entries.code: proc `[]=`[T](deq: var Deque[T]; i: Natural; val: sink T) {.inline.}
entries.description: Sets the i-th element of deq to val.

entries.code: proc `[]`[T](deq: Deque[T]; i: BackwardsIndex): lent T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Accesses the backwards indexed i-th element.

deq[^1] is the last element.

entries.code: proc `[]`[T](deq: var Deque[T]; i: BackwardsIndex): var T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Accesses the backwards indexed i-th element and returns a mutable reference to it.

deq[^1] is the last element.

entries.code: proc `[]=`[T](deq: var Deque[T]; i: BackwardsIndex; x: sink T) {.inline.}
entries.description:
Sets the backwards indexed i-th element of deq to x.

deq[^1] is the last element.

entries.name: items
entries.code: iterator items[T](deq: Deque[T]): lent T
entries.description:
Yields every element of deq.

See also:

  • mitems iterator

entries.name: mitems
entries.code: iterator mitems[T](deq: var Deque[T]): var T
entries.description:
Yields every element of deq, which can be modified.

See also:

  • items iterator

entries.name: pairs
entries.code: iterator pairs[T](deq: Deque[T]): tuple[key: int, val: T]
entries.description: Yields every (position, value)-pair of deq.

entries.name: contains
entries.code: proc contains[T](deq: Deque[T]; item: T): bool {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns true if item is in deq or false if not found.

Usually used via the in operator. It is the equivalent of deq.find(item) >= 0.

entries.name: addFirst
entries.code: proc addFirst[T](deq: var Deque[T]; item: sink T)
entries.description:
Adds an item to the beginning of deq.

See also:

  • addLast proc

entries.name: addLast
entries.code: proc addLast[T](deq: var Deque[T]; item: sink T)
entries.description:
Adds an item to the end of deq.

See also:

  • addFirst proc

entries.name: toDeque
entries.code: proc toDeque[T](x: openArray[T]): Deque[T]
entries.description:
Creates a new deque that contains the elements of x (in the same order).

See also:

  • initDeque proc

entries.name: peekFirst
entries.code: proc peekFirst[T](deq: Deque[T]): lent T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns the first element of deq, but does not remove it from the deque.

See also:

  • peekFirst proc which returns a mutable reference
  • peekLast proc

entries.name: peekLast
entries.code: proc peekLast[T](deq: Deque[T]): lent T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns the last element of deq, but does not remove it from the deque.

See also:

  • peekLast proc which returns a mutable reference
  • peekFirst proc

entries.name: peekFirst
entries.code: proc peekFirst[T](deq: var Deque[T]): var T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns a mutable reference to the first element of deq, but does not remove it from the deque.

See also:

  • peekFirst proc
  • peekLast proc

entries.name: peekLast
entries.code: proc peekLast[T](deq: var Deque[T]): var T {.inline.}
entries.description:
Returns a mutable reference to the last element of deq, but does not remove it from the deque.

See also:

  • peekFirst proc
  • peekLast proc

entries.name: popFirst
entries.code: proc popFirst[T](deq: var Deque[T]): T {.inline, discardable.}
entries.description:
Removes and returns the first element of the deq.

See also:

  • popLast proc
  • shrink proc

entries.name: popLast
entries.code: proc popLast[T](deq: var Deque[T]): T {.inline, discardable.}
entries.description:
Removes and returns the last element of the deq.

See also:

  • popFirst proc
  • shrink proc

entries.name: clear
entries.code: proc clear[T](deq: var Deque[T]) {.inline.}
entries.description:
Resets the deque so that it is empty.

See also:

  • shrink proc

entries.name: shrink
entries.code: proc shrink[T](deq: var Deque[T]; fromFirst = 0; fromLast = 0)
entries.description:
Removes fromFirst elements from the front of the deque and fromLast elements from the back.

If the supplied number of elements exceeds the total number of elements in the deque, the deque will remain empty.

See also:

  • clear proc
  • popFirst proc
  • popLast proc

entries.code: proc `$`[T](deq: Deque[T]): string
entries.description: Turns a deque into its string representation.

entries.code: func `==`[T](deq1, deq2: Deque[T]): bool
entries.description: The == operator for Deque. Returns true if both deques contains the same values in the same order.

entries.name: hash
entries.code: func hash[T](deq: Deque[T]): Hash
entries.description: Hashing of Deque.
